text.title: css-in-js-advanced Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for css-in-js-advanced as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Defining styled.div or recipe() calls inside a React component render body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates a new component class or stylesheet on every render, causing style cache misses and performance regressions. Move styled components and recipes to module scope; pass dynamic values via CSS variables or transient props.

failure-mode.description: Using vars: { color: '#3b82f6' } in vanilla-extract instead of createVar() + assignVars.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inline var assignment bypasses the typed contract; switching themes requires touching every .css.ts file instead of reassigning variables at the theme boundary. Use createVar() to decouple style definitions from runtime theme values.

failure-mode.description: Omitting defaultVariants in a recipe() definition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When a consumer calls button({ color: undefined }), vanilla-extract throws at runtime because no fallback exists for the undefined variant slot. Always provide sensible defaults.

failure-mode.description: Adding Emotion to Next.js App Router without the 'use client' CacheProvider wrapper.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Emotion runtime requires a client context; without the wrapper, the css prop silently degrades to a plain style attribute and FOUC occurs on first paint. Styling appears to work in development but fails in production or on cold cache.

failure-mode.description: Importing app-level modules (environment checks, API clients) from .css.ts files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: vanilla-extract runs .css.ts in a Node build sandbox; browser globals and async imports are unavailable and crash the build with cryptic "cannot find module" errors. Keep .css.ts pure and dependency-free of runtime code.

failure-mode.description: Memoizing css template literal calls instead of lifting styled components to module scope.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Memoization reduces re-computation but the component class is still created inside the component function; only module-scope definitions produce stable class names and prevent style churn.

failure-mode.description: Copying RecipeVariants<typeof button> but forgetting the matching defaultVariants.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The recipe type is inferred from variants but requires a defaultVariants object that matches the variant map. Agents often copy types without realizing the runtime dependency on defaults.
